```{r, echo=FALSE, results="hide"}
set.seed(sample.int(.Machine$integer.max, 1))

## Parâmetros aleatórios
theta <- sample(c(1, 2, 3, 4, 5), 1)
n     <- sample(c(25, 50, 100), 1)
sum_x <- round(n / theta + runif(1, -2, 2), 2)

## Resposta correta
correct <- round(n / sum_x, 4)

## Alternativas incorretas plausíveis
w1 <- round(sum_x / n, 4)
w2 <- round(n / sum_x + 1 / n, 4)
w3 <- round(1 / sum_x, 4)
w4 <- round(n^2 / sum_x^2, 4)

## Garantir unicidade
if (w1 == correct) w1 <- round(sum_x / n + 0.01, 4)
if (w2 == correct) w2 <- round(n / sum_x + 0.1, 4)
if (w2 == w1)      w2 <- round(n / sum_x - 0.1, 4)
if (w3 == correct || w3 == w1 || w3 == w2) w3 <- round(n / (sum_x + 1), 4)
if (w4 == correct || w4 == w1 || w4 == w2 || w4 == w3) w4 <- round(n / sum_x * 0.9, 4)

## Embaralhamento SEMPRE
all_vals <- c(correct, w1, w2, w3, w4)
# Garante cinco alternativas numericamente distintas após a parametrização.
integer_choices <- all(abs(all_vals - round(all_vals)) < sqrt(.Machine$double.eps))
choice_step <- if (integer_choices) 1 else max(1e-4, max(abs(all_vals), na.rm = TRUE) * 0.01)
while (anyDuplicated(all_vals)) {
  duplicate_position <- which(duplicated(all_vals))[1]
  all_vals[duplicate_position] <- max(all_vals, na.rm = TRUE) + choice_step
}
choices <- sample(all_vals)
sol_pos <- which(choices == correct)
sol_str <- paste(as.integer(seq_along(choices) == sol_pos), collapse = "")
```

Question
========

Seja $X_i \overset{\text{iid}}{\sim} \text{Exp}(\theta)$ com função de densidade $f(x;\theta) = \theta e^{-\theta x}$, $x > 0$, de modo que $E(X) = 1/\theta$. O estimador pelo método dos momentos obtém $\hat{\theta}$ igualando $m_1 = \bar{X} = 1/\hat{\theta}$, resultando em $\hat{\theta}_{MM} = n/\sum X_i$. Dado $n = `r n`$ e $\sum_{i=1}^{`r n`} X_i = `r sum_x`$, calcule $\hat{\theta}_{MM}$.

Answerlist
----------

* `r choices[1]`
* `r choices[2]`
* `r choices[3]`
* `r choices[4]`
* `r choices[5]`

Solution
========

Para $X_i \sim \text{Exp}(\theta)$, o primeiro momento teórico é $\mu_1 = E(X) = 1/\theta$. Igualando ao momento amostral $m_1 = \bar{X}$:
$$\bar{X} = \frac{1}{\hat{\theta}} \implies \hat{\theta}_{MM} = \frac{1}{\bar{X}} = \frac{n}{\sum_{i=1}^n X_i} = \frac{`r n`}{`r sum_x`} = `r correct`.$$

Meta-information
================
exname: Cap3S1Q3_emm_exponencial
extype: schoice
exsolution: `r sol_str`
exshuffle: FALSE
